blocks.text: Great! Now I'll run DESeq2 in R:

input.code:
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)

# Read counts and metadata
counts <- read.csv(file.path(Sys.getenv('WORK_DIR'), 'counts_de.csv'), row.names=1, check.names=FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv(file.path(Sys.getenv('WORK_DIR'), 'metadata_de.csv'), row.names=1)

# Convert counts to matrix and ensure integer type
counts <- as.matrix(counts)
storage.mode(counts) <- "integer"

print(paste("Counts matrix:", nrow(counts), "genes ×", ncol(counts), "cells"))
print(paste("Metadata:", nrow(metadata), "cells"))
print(table(metadata$cell_type))

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = counts,
    colData = metadata,
    design = ~ cell_type
)

# Set reference level (monocyte as baseline)
dds$cell_type <- relevel(dds$cell_type, ref = "Monocyte")
print(paste("Cell type levels:", paste(levels(dds$cell_type), collapse=", ")))

# Pre-filter: genes with >= 10 counts in >= 3 cells
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 3
dds <- dds[keep, ]

print(paste("After filtering: ", nrow(dds), "genes remaining"))
